element_introduction_order.element: Basic movement and autonomous lemming behavior
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn lemming pathfinding and basic traversal before skill assignment
Foundational skills like Builder, Miner, Blocker provide core puzzle vocabulary across all tribes
Players learn to overcome vertical and horizontal obstacles; enables access to new areas
Players construct bridges and barriers; critical for creating safe passages
Players learn destruction mechanics to clear paths and disable hazards

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early worlds (Blocker in Classic tribe; Attractor in subsequent worlds)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players control lemming flow and grouping; enables puzzle solving through crowd management
Unlimited fan tool propels lemmings with Ballooner/Surfer skills and powers mechanisms
Visual variety; no mechanical impact but signals thematic progression
Specialized skills required for complex puzzles; encourages strategic skill pool management

element_introduction_order.first_appears: End of each world (gold medal requirement)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Meta-progression goal: collecting 12 pieces unlocks true game completion

typical_relief_triggers: Fast-forward feature compression of puzzle solving
Reset/nuke option removes pressure of failed attempts
typical_tension_triggers: Time limit pressure within each level
Limited skill pool per level requires careful allocation
Hazards and traps threaten lemming survival
Multiple lemmings required for collaborative solutions
Gold medal requirement (deliver multiple lemmings, not just one)
Backtracking through earlier levels to preserve lemming count
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Flexible, player-controlled pacing within worlds; tribes system provides relief by allowing non-linear world selection when stuck; fast-forward feature reduces time pressure frustration

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Tribe selection order is player-determined; no prescribed difficulty curve path

difficulty_curve.early_game: Lower skill requirements; basic obstacle avoidance and simple terrain manipulation; safe introduction contexts dominate
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased skill pool complexity; introduction of direct player control (fan tool) and advanced tribe-specific mechanics; moderate-to-dangerous contexts
difficulty_curve.late_game: Complex multi-skill puzzle chains; requires lemming preservation across multiple levels; high collaboration requirements for gold medals
notable_spikes: Transition from single-lemming-to-exit requirement to gold medal multi-lemming preservation demands
Introduction of each new tribe's thematic skill variations
Talisman piece collection: final world complexities when completing all 12 worlds

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels require only one lemming to reach exit (bronze/silver achievable); gold medals demand preservation of multiple lemmings, creating two solution paths with different difficulty tiers
Each tribe emphasizes certain skills more frequently, but no skill is exclusive to one tribe; creates slight mechanical flavor variation without forcing rigid progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Highland vs. Cavelem vs. Circus tribe worlds

notable_level_patterns.description: Some levels require multiple lemmings working in sequence or synchronously; forces earlier level replays to maintain sufficient lemming population
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late world levels (specific examples not documented)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels featuring Ballooner, Surfer, or wind-powered mechanisms introduce direct player control as puzzle element; represents mid-game mechanical depth increase
